title: Keysight Design Engineering Software at DAC 2026: Going Deep on Chiplets, RF and AI-Driven Design - Semiwiki
gist: Keysight's Design Engineering Software is bringing surrogate-modeling/digital-twin agentic RF workflows, die-to-die signal-integrity tools for advanced packaging, ML-driven compact model extraction and RTL glitch-power analysis (claimed 10-30% dynamic power cuts) to DAC 2026, plus a quantum-EDA panel.

insights.claim: Keysight posters claim early RTL-level glitch-power detection with delay-aware analysis can cut dynamic power 10-30% before layout ever begins.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A quantified shift-left power-optimization claim; meaningful for power-constrained AI/mobile SoCs where dynamic power is a first-order design metric.
insights.quote: showing how early detection and delay-aware analysis can cut dynamic power 10-30% before layout ever begins
insights.locator: Detecting Glitch power in RTL code

insights.claim: Keysight is presenting surrogate modeling, digital twins and closed-loop validation to accelerate agentic RF/microwave design workflows built on SystemVue and ADS.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Surrogate models and digital twins are the mechanism by which AI compresses expensive RF simulation, a growing EDA-AI application area.
insights.quote: surrogate modeling, digital twins and closed-loop validation
insights.locator: Accelerating Agentic Design Workflows with Surrogate Modeling

insights.claim: Keysight introduces a machine-learning-driven flow for automatic compact model extraction aimed at replacing manual, expert-tuned processes with scalable, repeatable optimization.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Automating expert-tuned model extraction is another example of AI displacing scarce specialist labor in the design flow.
insights.quote: a machine-learning-driven flow for automatic compact model extraction, aimed at replacing manual, expert-tuned processes

insights.claim: Keysight is fielding a 'Build vs Buy: Who Owns the Intelligence Behind Tomorrow's Chips?' panel on managing and curating data for EDA AI workflows, and demos SOS Enterprise for governed, AI-ready engineering data at scale.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Governed engineering data management is emerging as a strategic prerequisite for EDA-AI, a build-vs-buy decision point for chip companies.
insights.quote: Build vs Buy: Who Owns the Intelligence Behind Tomorrow’s Chips?
insights.locator: Managing and Curating Data for EDA AI workflows

insights.claim: Keysight is addressing chiplet, 3DIC and 3D-HI interconnect challenges with mixed-domain modeling for hatched ground planes in die-to-die interconnects, validated on silicon-bridge and mobile-PCB test vehicles.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Die-to-die signal-integrity tooling rides the chiplet/advanced-packaging adoption wave central to AI-silicon scaling.
insights.locator: Die-to-Die Signal Integrity for Advanced Packaging

insights.claim: Keysight is spotlighting quantum computing with a 'From Bit to Qubit: EDA Meets Quantum' panel examining how quantum EDA platforms and digital twins intersect with chip-design workflows.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Signals EDA vendors extending toolchains toward quantum hardware, an early-stage adjacent market to monitor.
insights.quote: From Bit to Qubit: EDA Meets Quantum
